hyperedges.label: Demo Scenarios to Knowledge Base Mapping

nodes.label: Safety Rules Policy
nodes.rationale: Safety rules defined early to prevent dangerous repair guidance for mains electricity, gas leaks, swollen batteries, smoke/fire, microwave internals, fuel leaks, carbon monoxide, shock risk, and high-voltage wiring; high-risk cases get external diagnosis only; critical-risk cases stop guidance entirely.

nodes.label: Core Repair Flow
nodes.rationale: Pipeline: image/symptom -> safety pre-check -> local RAG -> structured diagnosis -> guided repair -> device memory. Offline-first design so no cloud dependency during inference.

nodes.label: Product Vision: Offline Multimodal Repair Copilot
nodes.norm_label: product vision: offline multimodal repair copilot

nodes.label: Repair Reasoning Loop
nodes.rationale: Observe->Ask->Diagnose->Verify->Repair->Confirm->Prevent loop chosen to minimize unnecessary questions while maintaining diagnostic accuracy and user confirmation at each step.

nodes.label: Risk Level Classification
nodes.rationale: Four tiers (Low, Medium, High, Critical) map directly to AI behavior: direct steps, steps with warnings, external-diagnosis-only, and full stop with emergency action respectively.

nodes.label: High-Level Architecture
nodes.rationale: Local app + local model runtime + local RAG + safety guardrails + repair-agent workflow. Architecture goal: run repair diagnosis and guidance locally with no cloud dependency during inference.

nodes.label: Output Schema (Structured JSON)
nodes.rationale: Strict schemas used so UI can render consistent cards and the safety layer can validate outputs before display.

nodes.label: Offline Deployment Levels
nodes.rationale: Four deployment levels (hackathon laptop, field laptop, edge companion, mobile) reflect progressive hardening from demo to production while keeping offline-first as the core invariant.
